content: Tobacco use assessment includes: (1) Screening—all patients at every visit (USPSTF Grade A recommendation); (2) Quantification—pack-years calculation (packs per day × years smoked), cigarettes per day, time to first cigarette (indicates addiction severity); (3) Dependence assessment—Fagerström Test for Nicotine Dependence evaluating time to first cigarette, difficulty refraining, morning craving; (4) Biomarkers—cotinine levels (serum, saliva, urine) for objective measurement, exhaled carbon monoxide for recent smoking; (5) Readiness to quit—stages of change model (precontemplation, contemplation, preparation, action, maintenance). Secondhand smoke exposure assessed through home/car rules, workplace policies. Youth screening includes e-cigarette use assessment (JUUL, vaping). Population surveillance uses BRFSS, NHIS, NYTS, and Global Adult Tobacco Survey.
